entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor L. Moench, a C4 annual grass known for its hardiness and drought characteristics.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand and grow in drought conditions.

entities.name: TROPICAL REGIONS
entities.description: Warm, humid regions typically found near the equator.

entities.name: MARGINAL SOILS
entities.description: Soils with low fertility or poor physical properties that limit agricultural productivity.

entities.name: RAPID GROWTH
entities.description: The capacity of a plant to develop and increase in size quickly.

entities.name: HIGH SUGAR CONTENT
entities.description: The presence of high levels of sugars, typically between 10-15%.

entities.name: HIGH BIOMASS
entities.description: The production of a large amount of organic plant material.

entities.name: ADAPTABILITY
entities.description: The ability of a crop to adjust to harsh agro-climatic conditions.

entities.name: METAL ABSORPTION
entities.description: The property of a plant to absorb metals from the environment.

entities.name: SWEET SORGHUM
entities.description: A variety of S. bicolor cultivated primarily for syrup, forage, and feed.

entities.name: GRAIN SORGHUM
entities.description: A variety of S. bicolor cultivated primarily for grain production.

entities.name: BIOFUEL FEEDSTOCK
entities.description: Biological material used as a raw material for the production of biofuels.

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which a plant utilizes water for growth and production.

entities.name: RADIATION USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which a plant converts solar radiation into biomass.

entities.name: BIO-PRODUCTS
entities.description: Products derived from biological resources, including food, feed, and fuel.

entities.name: BIO-ETHANOL
entities.description: A renewable fuel produced from the fermentation of sugars in biomass.

entities.name: PHYTOREMEDIATION
entities.description: The use of plants to remove, transfer, or stabilize contaminants in soil and groundwater.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns that affect agriculture.

entities.name: GENETICS
entities.description: The study of genes and heredity to improve crop characteristics.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The science of producing plants with desired traits through selective mating.

entities.name: BIOTECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The application of biological systems and organisms to develop or make products.

entities.name: PROCESSING
entities.description: The techniques used to convert raw crop materials into usable products.
